task_objective: Increase frontend diagnostic coverage so plugin logs explain QAM state, user actions, settings persistence, launcher outcomes, and lifecycle-source behavior.
design_decisions: Structured UI events use stable event names and sorted key-value fields.
Logs are emitted at action and controller boundaries rather than during React renders or high-frequency polling.
Settings diagnostics distinguish requested, queued, persisted, superseded, rejected, skipped, and rolled-back outcomes.
The frontend logging transport routes to the matching browser console level and contains backend log RPC failures locally.
Diagnostic fields avoid full settings payloads, environment data, and full checksums.
results: Added structured diagnostics for plugin and QAM lifecycle, initial loading and cache selection, manual refresh and operations, log modals, settings persistence, Ludusavi launching, and Steam lifecycle-source setup and failures.
validation: Passed tracked-file ruff check and format, ty check, 396 Python tests, 69 frontend tests, TypeScript type checking, Rollup build, and the complete pnpm supply-chain verification with no known vulnerabilities.
